name: Tipsy Cake (an Old Southern Form of Trifle)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup toasted almonds or pecans, chopped
1-2 tablespoons sherry, bourbon, or scuppernong wine (or substitute)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare homemade boiled custard using your preferred method. Ensure the custard is thick and stir in enough gelatin to make it congeal. Let cool slightly.
2. Break the angel food cake into small pieces and dip each piece into the alcohol of choice. Place a layer of soaked cake pieces in a large bowl, covering with toasted nuts and a sprinkle of nutmeg.
3. Add a layer of custard, then repeat layers until the bowl is full. Refrigerate for at least one day (preferably two).
4. Before serving, whip the cream and spoon over the top. Optionally, add a dash of nutmeg and a maraschino cherry on top.
